content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Hamburgers". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/371/problem/C
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of buns, then the next line contains an integer m, which is the number of cutlets, and the next line contains an integer k, which is the number of plates. Then there are k lines, each containing the price of a plate.
content.outputFormat: The minimum amount of money that is needed to buy the required number of hamburgers.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the prices of the plates and then select the cheapest plates until we have enough hamburgers.

code.Python3:
import sys
n, m, k = map(int, input().split())
prices = sorted([int(input()) for _ in range(k)])
sum = 0
for i in range(min(n, m)):
    sum += prices[i]
print(sum)
